title: Senior Doctors in England Vote for Potential Strikes
subtitle: Consultants express frustration over pay and working conditions, creating new pressure for the government.

summary.en: Consultant doctors in England have voted in favor of taking industrial action over pay and pension concerns. The British Medical Association (BMA) reports that 76% of those who voted support potential strikes. Health Secretary James Murray has criticized the decision, stating that consultants are already among the highest-paid public sector workers. While the vote gives the BMA a mandate to organize strikes for the next year, no specific dates have been announced yet. This development follows a recent agreement that ended a long-running dispute with resident doctors.
summary.zh: 英格蘭的顧問醫師已投票贊成針對薪資與退休金問題採取勞工行動。英國醫學會報告指出，百分之七十六的投票者支持潛在的罷工。衛生大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞批評此決定，稱顧問醫師已是公共部門中薪資最高的族群之一。雖然投票賦予了英國醫學會未來一年組織罷工的授權，但目前尚未宣布具體日期。此發展發生在近期與住院醫師結束長期爭議的協議之後。

paragraphs.en: Consultant doctors across England have voted in favor of taking industrial action—a term for strikes or other protests by workers—over concerns regarding their pay and pensions. According to the British Medical Association (BMA), 76% of the senior doctors who participated in the recent ballot said they would be willing to strike. This result provides the BMA with a mandate, or formal permission from its members, to organize strikes over the next 12 months.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭各地的顧問醫師已投票贊成採取勞工行動（即罷工或其他抗議活動），以表達對薪資與退休金問題的擔憂。根據英國醫學會（British Medical Association, BMA）指出，在近期參與投票的資深醫師中，有百分之七十六表示願意罷工。此結果賦予了英國醫學會一項授權，即獲得會員的正式許可，可在未來十二個月內組織罷工。

paragraphs.en: This decision marks a new challenge for the National Health Service (NHS). The vote comes just one week after resident doctors, formerly known as junior doctors, accepted a new pay deal with the government to end three years of industrial action. While the consultants have voted to support strikes, they have not yet announced any specific dates for walkouts. This choice offers a small window of hope that further talks with the government could prevent the need for actual strikes.
paragraphs.zh: 這項決定對英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）構成了新的挑戰。此次投票發生在住院醫師（先前稱為實習醫師）與政府達成新的薪資協議、結束長達三年勞工行動的一週後。儘管顧問醫師已投票支持罷工，但尚未宣布任何具體的停工日期。此選擇提供了一絲希望，顯示與政府的進一步談判或許能避免實際罷工的發生。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Helen Neary and Dr. Shanu Datta, co-chairs of the BMA consultants committee, stated that the vote sends a clear message. They noted that consultants are no longer willing to accept what they describe as a continued attack on their pay and professional value. The BMA argues that average pay for consultants is still 26% lower in real terms than it was 17 years ago. Beyond pay, consultants are also asking for a shorter working week, moving from 40 hours down to 37.5 hours, and more time to focus on developing new ways to improve patient care.
paragraphs.zh: 英國醫學會顧問委員會共同主席海倫·尼里（Helen Neary）醫師與夏努·達塔（Shanu Datta）醫師表示，這次投票傳達了明確的訊息。他們指出，顧問醫師不再願意接受他們所稱的對薪資與專業價值的持續打壓。英國醫學會主張，顧問醫師的平均薪資實質價值與十七年前相比，仍低了百分之二十六。除了薪資外，顧問醫師也要求縮短每週工時，從四十小時減至三十七點五小時，並爭取更多時間專注於開發改善病患照護的新方法。

paragraphs.en: However, the government has strongly disagreed with these claims. Health and Social Care Secretary James Murray said there is "no justification" for strikes. He pointed out that consultants are already among the top 2% of earners in the country. According to BBC Health, the average consultant in England earns over £152,000 a year. Mr. Murray noted that basic starting pay for these doctors has increased by 28.5% over the last four years. He urged the BMA and the consultants not to rush into a new cycle of disruptive action, arguing that it would only get in the way of important work to modernize contracts and improve job planning.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，政府強烈反對這些說法。衛生與社會照護大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞（James Murray）表示，罷工「毫無正當理由」。他指出，顧問醫師已是國內收入最高的百分之二族群。根據英國廣播公司健康頻道（BBC Health）報導，英格蘭顧問醫師的平均年薪超過十五萬兩千英鎊。莫瑞先生提到，這些醫師的基本起薪在過去四年內已調升百分之二十八點五。他敦促英國醫學會與顧問醫師不要倉促進入新一輪的干擾性行動，並主張這只會阻礙合約現代化與改善工作規劃的重要工作。

paragraphs.en: The Guardian reported that the ballot saw a turnout of 51.5%, which is just above the 50% threshold required by law for a strike to be valid. Out of 35,067 eligible members, 18,069 voted, with 13,695 supporting the potential for strikes. Meanwhile, a separate ballot for specialist, associate specialist, and specialty (SAS) doctors failed to reach the legal turnout requirement, even though 90% of those who did vote supported strike action.
paragraphs.zh: 《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，此次投票率為百分之五十一點五，僅略高於法律規定罷工有效的百分之五十門檻。在三萬五千零六十七名合格會員中，有一萬八千零六十九人投票，其中一萬三千六百九十五人支持罷工。與此同時，針對專科醫師、副專科醫師與特殊專科醫師（SAS）的另一場投票，儘管參與投票者中有百分之九十支持罷工，卻因未達法定投票率門檻而無效。

paragraphs.en: If consultants do decide to walk out, it will create significant difficulties for hospital managers. While consultants will continue to provide emergency and urgent care, a strike would likely lead to the cancellation of many planned operations and appointments that require senior doctors. In the past, the NHS has become quite skilled at managing services during strike days by moving staff around to cover gaps. However, hospital leaders are concerned about the impact of another period of uncertainty.
paragraphs.zh: 若顧問醫師決定停工，將為醫院管理者帶來重大困難。雖然顧問醫師仍會提供緊急與急迫照護，但罷工很可能導致許多需要資深醫師參與的預定手術與門診被迫取消。過去，英國國家醫療保健服務在罷工期間透過調度人力填補空缺，已累積相當豐富的服務管理經驗。然而，醫院領導階層仍對另一段不確定時期的影響感到擔憂。

paragraphs.en: The situation remains tense as both sides look toward the future. The BMA has emphasized that no strikes need to take place if the government addresses their concerns. On the other hand, the government has made it clear that they will not revisit the 3.5% pay rise already offered for the 2026/27 period. For now, patients and hospital staff are waiting to see if the government and the BMA can reach an agreement before any disruption begins. The outcome of these discussions will be critical for the stability of the NHS in the coming months.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著雙方展望未來，局勢依然緊張。英國醫學會強調，若政府能解決他們的擔憂，就不需要進行罷工。另一方面，政府已明確表示，不會重新審議已為二零二六至二零二七年度提出的百分之三點五加薪方案。目前，病患與醫院員工正等待政府與英國醫學會能否在干擾發生前達成協議。這些討論的結果，對於未來幾個月英國國家醫療保健服務的穩定至關重要。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An act of leaving a place of work as a form of protest or strike.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 罷工；（因抗議而）集體離職。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常被誤認為是動詞短語，這裡作為名詞使用，指代罷工行動。文中：While the consultants have voted to support strikes, they have not yet announced any specific dates for walkouts.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Values adjusted for inflation to show the actual purchasing power of money.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 實質價值（扣除通貨膨脹因素後的價值）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個經濟學慣用語，容易被誤解為「真實的條件」。文中：The BMA argues that average pay for consultants is still 26% lower in real terms than it was 17 years ago.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prevent or delay the progress of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語意為「擋路」，引申為阻礙某事進行。文中：He urged the BMA and the consultants not to rush into a new cycle of disruptive action, arguing that it would only get in the way of important work to modernize contracts and improve job planning.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To leave one's job as a form of protest.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於日常對話（走出去），在此處專指罷工。文中：If consultants do decide to walk out, it will create significant difficulties for hospital managers.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: Consultants in England vote in favour of NHS strikes

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Consultant doctors in England vote for NHS strikes over pay and working week

previousArticles.title: Senior Doctors in England Vote for Potential Strikes
previousArticles.summaryEn: Consultant doctors in England have voted in favor of taking industrial action over pay and pension concerns. The British Medical Association (BMA) reports that 76% of those who voted support potential strikes. Health Secretary James Murray has criticized the decision, stating there is no justification for such action. While a mandate for strikes now exists, no specific dates have been announced yet. This development follows a recent agreement between the government and resident doctors to end their own long-running dispute.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the specific reason why the SAS doctors' ballot did not result in a mandate for strike action?

options.A: The majority of SAS doctors voted against the strike.
options.B: The total number of votes cast did not meet the legal turnout requirement.
options.C: The BMA decided not to organize strikes for this specific group of doctors.
options.D: The government reached a pay agreement with them before the ballot concluded.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，雖然有 90% 的 SAS 醫生支持罷工，但該投票未達到法律規定的 50% 投票率門檻，因此無法發動罷工。選項 A 錯誤，因為 90% 的投票者是支持的。選項 C 錯誤，因為這是法律門檻問題而非工會決策。選項 D 為無中生有的資訊。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the BMA have chosen not to announce specific dates for walkouts immediately after the vote?

options.A: They are waiting for the government to officially recognize the 51.5% turnout.
options.B: They want to maintain pressure on the government while keeping a path open for negotiation.
options.C: The law requires a cooling-off period of at least one month after a ballot.
options.D: They need more time to organize the logistics of emergency care coverage.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段提到，顧問醫生尚未宣布罷工日期，這提供了一個「希望之窗」，讓政府有機會透過對話避免罷工。這顯示 BMA 希望利用罷工授權作為談判籌碼，而非立刻執行。選項 A、C、D 皆未在文中提及，屬於過度推論或與事實不符。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'justification' mean in the context of the Health Secretary's statement?

options.A: A legal document proving employment status.
options.B: A valid reason or acceptable explanation for an action.
options.C: A financial adjustment to cover inflation costs.
options.D: A formal apology for past mistakes.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中，衛生大臣認為醫生罷工是沒有「justification」（正當理由/辯解）的，因為顧問醫生的薪資水平已經很高。選項 A、C、D 皆不符合該詞在文中的語境。此處的 justification 指的是為何採取罷工行動的合理性依據。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the current situation between the NHS consultants and the government?

options.A: The government has agreed to all of the consultants' demands to prevent a total shutdown of the NHS.
options.B: Consultants have officially started their strike, causing widespread cancellation of surgeries.
options.C: Both parties have reached a compromise, and the threat of industrial action has been completely removed.
options.D: Consultants have authorized strike action due to pay and working conditions, but negotiations are still ongoing.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。這篇文章的主旨是報導顧問醫生投票支持罷工的結果，以及雙方對於薪資、工作時數的爭議與談判僵局。選項 A 錯誤，政府拒絕重談薪資；選項 B 錯誤，目前尚未宣布罷工日期；選項 C 錯誤，雙方仍處於緊張對峙狀態，尚未達成協議。
